metadata.name: Gulf Briefing
metadata.description: Daily news briefing covering all 6 GCC states + Yemen for an NYT correspondent

all.headline: Key takeaways from Trump’s war on Iran meeting
all.description: US President Donald Trump held a cabinet meeting where he discussed the war on Iran with senior officials.

all.headline: Trump says Iran ‘begging’ for deal to end war as Tehran issues new demands
all.description: Trump says US, Israel wiped out navy and air force, as Iran lawmakers plan to collect tolls for transiting ships.

all.headline: Kim Jong Un gifted gun after Belarus friendship treaty
all.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko gifted North Korean leader Kim Jong Un a gun.

all.headline: Iranian missiles cause widespread damage across Israel in latest wave
all.description: Iranian missiles have caused widespread damage across Israel in the latest wave of attacks.

all.headline: More than one million displaced by Israel’s evacuations in Lebanon
all.description: More than 250,000 people flee Lebanon in two weeks, with 125,000 crossing into Syria, including 7,000 Lebanese citizens.

all.headline: London police officer filmed in crowd intimidating Al Jazeera journalists
all.description: Metropolitan Police says reviewing footage of incident, reporters must be able to work 'without intimidation'.

all.headline: LIVE: Brazil vs France – FIFA World Cup 2026 warm up
all.description: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, before our comprehensive text commentary stream.

all.headline: Details revealed of Board of Peace plan for Gaza disarmament
all.description: Plan, submitted by Board of Peace head Nikolay Mladenov, calls for gradual disarmament over eight months.

all.headline: Pentagon boss claims Iran now has ‘no navy, no navy leader’
all.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has said that Iran war is 'stuff for the history books'.

all.headline: Olympics align with Trump as transgender athletes banned from women’s games
all.description: Transgender athletes banned from women's Olympic events by new IOC policy aiming for 'fairness, safety and integrity'.

all.headline: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Saudi Arabia for 'important meetings' - Reuters
Iran war damage to Qatar hits global LNG outlook, upends Asia demand growth - Reuters
Nine dead, 45 missing after shipwreck off Djibouti, IOM says - Reuters
US military says it struck vessel in Caribbean, killed four people - Reuters
Oil prices rise 5% as investors fear further Middle East escalation - Reuters
U.S. envoy Witkoff says Iran is seeking an off-ramp - Reuters
Philippines launches $333 million fund to boost fuel security - Reuters
Panel wants prosecution of ousted Nepal PM over violence in 'Gen Z' protests - Reuters
Iran war chokes petrochemical supply, sends plastic prices soaring - Reuters
Pakistan resumes military operations against Afghanistan - Reuters
Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia as Ukraine provides expertise against Iranian drones - AP News
Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as an emboldened Russia starts spring offensive - AP News
What we know about diplomatic efforts to end the Iran war - AP News
EU lawmakers approve trade deal with US but add safeguards - AP News
How the war in Iran is landing with Republicans, according to a new AP-NORC poll - AP News
Stocks rise and oil prices ease as Wall Street keeps yo-yoing because of the war with Iran - AP News
Iran war - AP News
Hawaii suffers its worst flooding in decades - AP News
JON GAMBRELL - AP News
Melania Trump brings humanoid robot to event to boost education and technology - AP News
Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
all.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

all.headline: Zelensky visits Saudi Arabia after offering Ukraine's drone expertise
all.description: There is renewed urgency for Ukraine to get a drone deal done, as the US increasingly focuses on Iran.

all.headline: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
all.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

all.headline: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
all.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

all.headline: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
all.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

all.headline: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
all.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

all.headline: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
all.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

all.headline: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
all.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

all.headline: Israel says it will take control of large buffer zone in southern Lebanon
all.description: Thousands of displaced Lebanese residents will not be allowed to return home until northern Israel is safe, Israel's defence minister says.

all.headline: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
all.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

screenshots.name: Saudi MFA
tweets: During the meeting, the latest developments in the regional and international situation and the efforts made in this regard were discussed.

The meeting was attended by His Excellency the Ambassador of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques to the Republic of France, Fahd bin Mayouf Al-Ruwaili, and His Excellency the Director General of the Office of His Highness the Minister, Walid bin Abdul Hamid Al-Ismail.
During the meeting, bilateral cooperation relations between the two countries were reviewed, in addition to discussing developments in regional situations of common interest.

screenshots.name: Prince Khalid bin Salman
tweets: In a call with Hungary’s Minister of Defense, Kristóf Szalay-Bobrovniczky, we discussed regional developments, including Iran's flagrant attacks on the Kingdom and other regional states, as well as their implications for regional and international security and stability.

I received a phone call from His Excellency the Minister of Defense of Hungary, Krzysztof Szálai Bobrovinsky.
We discussed current developments in the region in light of the continued blatant Iranian attacks on the Kingdom and a number of countries in the region and their repercussions on regional and international security and stability.

I met with Her Excellency the French Minister of the Armed Forces, Catherine Vautrin.
We reviewed the bilateral relations between our two friendly countries in the defense field and ways to strengthen them, discussed developments in the current situation in the region and their repercussions at various levels, and condemned the repeated Iranian attacks targeting the Kingdom.

We thank God Almighty for honoring us by completing the fast and prayer of the blessed month of Ramadan. We ask God to preserve our security and stability, and to protect our brave heroes on the borders and borders in the various military and civilian sectors. 
Happy new year, and may our country be proud and elevated.

screenshots.name: Prince Faisal bin Farhan

All Federal government departments will move to the new weekend from January 1, 2022.
Today, the Federal Supreme Council unanimously elected His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan as President of the United Arab Emirates.

The Ministry of Presidential Affairs mourns to the people of the #Emirates, the Arab and Islamic nations, and the entire world, the leader of the nation and the shepherd of its path, His Highness Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the President of the State, who moved to the side of his Lord, content and satisfactory, today, Friday, May 13.

#UAE

A patent was granted by the Ministry of Economy for an innovative and promising stem cell treatment for infections with the emerging #Coronavirus. This treatment was developed by a team of doctors and researchers at the Abu Dhabi Stem Cell Center and includes extracting stem cells from the patient’s blood and reintroducing them after activation.
#The UAE

screenshots.name: Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid
tweets:
Happy New Year to all of our mothers.. Happy New Year to mothers, a source of trust, a teacher for generations, and a creator of values. 

A tribute of appreciation and gratitude to the mothers of martyrs, the mothers of heroes in the fields of sacrifice, and the mothers of men and women who make a difference every day in our homelands. 

May God protect the Emirates...and may God protect mothers in every corner of the country

Happy New Year to our country... Happy New Year to our Gulf... Happy New Year to the Islamic nation... 
We ask God to accept good deeds from us and from you... and to return them to us and you while we are in safety, security and reassurance... stability, prosperity and tranquility... 
May God protect the Emirates... May God protect the Gulf... May God protect the Arab and Islamic nation

With the conclusions of the holy month, we conclude the “Limit Life” campaign that we launched during the blessed month with the aim of saving five million children from death due to hunger around the world and with the aim of preventing another 30 million children from slipping into malnutrition that leads to death.. 

Thanks to God and his success, the campaign was able to achieve more than 2.8 billion dirhams

Childhood is the first school in which a person learns the meanings of life, the values of society, and the principles of morals. What we sow and instill in our children today is what we reap and reap tomorrow in our homelands. 
In childhood, we draw the picture we want for the future of our countries. A healthy childhood is a great national responsibility on everyone’s shoulders.

My sincere condolences and sympathy to the brotherly Omani people and to my brother, His Majesty Sultan Haitham bin Tariq, may God protect him, on the death of Mr. Fahd bin Mahmoud Al Said, may God rest his soul... who represented the Sultanate well through many years of political and governmental work.  
May God have mercy on him and place him in spacious gardens.

screenshots.name: Anwar Gargash
tweets: The provocative statement of the Turkish Defense Minister represents a new downfall for his country’s diplomacy. The logic of the Sublime Porte, the Supreme State, and its decrees has its place in the historical archive. Relations are not managed by threats and intimidation, and there is no place for colonial illusions in this time. It is best for Türkiye to stop its interference in Arab affairs.
Busy in the Emirates. The Emirates is busy with the future.
In the Emirates, we chose honesty and transparency, we chose stability over chaos, we chose moderation and development, we chose trust and clarity, and we chose Salman and Saudi Arabia.

The Brotherhood’s organized campaign against Egypt and its stability has failed miserably. The directed and supported media platforms are matched by real popular support for the Egyptian state and its institutions. Egypt is recovering and facing challenges with daily determination, and the reality is different from the one promoted by this foreign-funded partisan media.
The media summary of one year since the Qatar crisis is that Saudi Twitter defeated Al Jazeera. Your voice is stronger and clearer when you defend your homeland.

On the occasion of the blessed Eid Al-Fitr, I am pleased to address the people of our great southern people inside and outside the country with my most sincere congratulations and blessings, asking the Lord to return it to our people and nation with goodness, blessings, victory and empowerment.

In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

God Almighty said: {Among the believers are men who have been true to what they covenanted with God. And among them are some who have fulfilled their promise, and among them are some who wait. And they have not changed it by any means.} God Almighty has spoken the truth.
With hearts that believe in God’s will and destiny, and with great sadness and deep sorrow, we mourn for our people
